title: albuca gethylloides
intro: Albuca gethylloides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, commonly known as the white arum or white medeema. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Albuca gethylloides include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antidote for snake bites and insect stings. Herbal preparations of Albuca gethylloides can be made into a poultice or a decoction, which can be applied topically or taken orally to leverage its medicinal properties.
